steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The passage introduces the idea that "invasive" and "native" are temporary labels that can shift with climate change. The blank must follow logically from that claim applied to Washington's classification of the American bullfrog.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — Washington was previously home to some American bullfrogs but they were outcompeted by invading species.: This invents historical backstory about the bullfrog being outcompeted. Nothing in the passage supports this. ✗
  • Choice B — Washington may eventually need to reexamine its designation of the American bullfrog as invasive.: If "invasive" is a temporary label that can change with climate, then a species currently classified as invasive could lose that designation. This follows directly. ✓
  • Choice C — it's appropriate for Washington to designate certain species as invasive but the state should designate the American bullfrog as native.: This claims the bullfrog should be reclassified as native right now, which goes beyond what the passage says. The passage only notes labels are temporary, not that the bullfrog has already crossed that threshold. ✗
  • Choice D — changes in Earth's climate are likely to result in the American bullfrog establishing itself as a native species in locations other than Washington.: This shifts the conclusion to other locations entirely, abandoning Washington, which is the subject of the passage. ✗
B is correct.
